articles.title: Trump’s 50% Tariffs on Canada: What to Know, and What’s Next
articles.description: The president said punishing 50 percent tariffs would begin by Aug. 19, a significant escalation of the trade war between Canada and the U.S. that he started over a year ago.

articles.title: Laredo’s Future Rides on Trump’s Next Trade Move
articles.description: The economy of Laredo, Texas, has boomed under the president’s global tariffs. Now, his demands may threaten its success.

articles.title: Speaking for Fallen Soldiers, Trump Says They Supported the Iran War
articles.description: President Trump attended a dignified transfer for the soldiers as Americans were growing increasingly critical of the war.

articles.title: Canadian Wildfire Evacuees Outraged Over Lacking Support and Communication
articles.description: Residents of northwestern Ontario say their warnings about fires they spotted were dismissed and official information as the flames spread often didn’t come at all.

articles.title: Global Oil Price Jumps as War Threats Intensify
articles.description: But stock investors shrug off Iran developments as they await crucial earnings reports from tech firms including Alphabet and Tesla.

articles.title: Trump’s Trade Negotiator Doubles Down on Tariffs Before Global Duties Expire
articles.description: Jamieson Greer, the president’s top trade negotiator, told the Senate that the United States remained intent on tariffs as it prepared to replace duties the Supreme Court struck down.

articles.title: James Woolsey, Embattled C.I.A. Director Under Clinton, Dies at 84
articles.description: He arrived in Washington a conservative Democrat and drifted rightward, supporting the 2003 invasion of Iraq and Donald J. Trump’s 2016 presidential bid.

articles.title: Homan blasts Dem hypocrisy on deportations, recalls past when 'Obama gave me an award' for enforcing border
articles.description: Tom Homan says he received an award from former President Barack Obama for record deportations, arguing Democrats only demonized border security due to hatred for Trump.

articles.title: Oskar Eustis Is Stepping Down From the Public Theater
articles.description: Eustis has been the Public Theater’s artistic director for 21 years, stewarding Free Shakespeare in the Park, nurturing “Hamilton” and navigating the coronavirus pandemic.

articles.title: Oil prices rise after Rubio says Iran is not serious about a deal
articles.description: The U.S. has launched strikes against Iran for 11 consecutive days, citing a need to "degrade Iran's ability to threaten commercial shipping."

articles.title: Vacation hot spot faces new CDC travel warning as outbreak cases continue climbing
articles.description: The CDC issued a Level 2 Travel Health Notice for Costa Rica's Guanacaste Province after chikungunya cases surfaced near popular Playa Langosta. Travelers were told to take precautions.

articles.title: Tariffs Will Long Outlive Trump
articles.description: We will be stuck with tariffs long after this president is out of office.

articles.title: ‘The Daily Show’ Tackles Trump’s Latest Move in His ‘War on Canada’
articles.description: “Fighting again with Canada?” Desi Lydic said about the president imposing new tariffs on the country. “I thought we were going to work this ‘oot.’”

articles.title: Kevin Warsh has homed in on three key phrases. How Fed watchers interpret them
articles.description: In five public appearances, Warsh has used the phrase "family fight" 13 times, returning to "first principles" 11 times and "inflation is a choice" for the Fed six times.

articles.title: Iran War Live Updates: Iran Digs In After Latest Trump Threat on Civilian Targets
articles.description: Early Thursday in the Middle East, the U.S. Central Command said it was carrying out a new wave strikes on Iran, a 12th consecutive day of attacks. Iranian state media said the province that is home to Iran’s only operating nuclear power plant had been hit in a missile strike.

articles.title: Houthis Claim Strikes on 2 Saudi Oil Tankers in Red Sea
articles.description: The Saudi authorities had yet to confirm the hits, which would be the first attacks since the Iranian-backed Yemeni group announced a blockade on Saudi Arabia.

articles.title: U.S. Signs Deal to Help Saudis Obtain Nuclear Power
articles.description: The accord is intended to further bind the United States with the Saudi government as the war with Iran strains the relationship.

articles.title: Mamdani Knew He Couldn’t Arrest Netanyahu. But He Could Denounce Him.
articles.description: In a video message, the mayor of New York City said he could not legally arrest Israel’s prime minister but reinforced his position as a voice of the international left.

articles.title: A Wartime Rift in Ukraine
articles.description: President Volodymyr Zelensky fired his top general after a feud between old-school officers and younger innovators spilled into the streets.

articles.title: With Saudi Deal, Trump Loosens Nuclear Standards for One Close Ally
articles.description: Nuclear experts say it throws fuel on the smoldering fire of atomic proliferation, creating a destabilizing situation that could raise the risk of nuclear war.

articles.title: Clarity Act Mired In Debate Over Whether to Bar President From Selling Crypto
articles.description: Democrats and Republicans are haggling about the Clarity Act, a major bill pushed by the crypto industry, as it moves closer to a Senate vote.

articles.title: Experts reveal how to avoid deadly Legionnaires' disease as NYC outbreak kills 5
articles.description: The New York City Legionnaires' disease outbreak has killed five and sickened 82, but doctors say key prevention steps can reduce exposure risk.

articles.title: A Soldier’s Death Overseas Stuns a Neighborhood in Queens
articles.description: Sgt. Angel Rampersad, 28, is believed to have been killed in an Iranian attack on Jordan last week. In Ozone Park, where her family lived, neighbors mourned and remembered her.

articles.title: Trump’s Plan for Science: More Money for A.I., Less for Universities
articles.description: Michael Kratsios, President Trump’s science adviser, proposed overhauling how the government funds research. Democrats said Mr. Trump’s actions had weakened science.

articles.title: The U.S. Signs a Nuclear Pact With Saudi Arabia
articles.description: Also, Trump threatens to retaliate against civilian targets in Iran.

articles.title: What is Pickaxe Mountain, the Iranian nuclear facility Trump is threatening to strike?
articles.description: "We'll be hitting that area pretty soon, and very heavily," President Donald Trump said about Iran's underground nuclear facility in Pickaxe Mountain.

articles.title: Tourists behave badly at Yellowstone’s largest hot spring
articles.description: Video captured two visitors illegally crossing the fragile crust of the Grand Prismatic Spring. “I thought I was going to watch people die," an onlooker said.

articles.title: Independent autopsy for Mississippi 18-year-old is inconclusive
articles.description: Local officials have said no foul play is suspected in Nolan Wells’s death during a boating trip. But an attorney for Wells’s family said the autopsy could not rule it out.

articles.title: ICE raids took economic toll in Los Angeles, report says
articles.description: Latino business owners recounted sharp drops — often more than 50 percent — in their revenue right after the raids, the researchers say.

articles.title: Stocks and the Economy Are Increasingly Relying on the A.I. Boom
articles.description: Investment in artificial intelligence and related companies is lifting the stock market and spending across the economy.

articles.title: WATCH: Squad Democrat speaks out after teen found shot dead at husband’s Boston property
articles.description: Yoseliani Marte, 18, was found dead from a gunshot wound inside a rental property owned by Rep. Ayanna Pressley's husband Conan Harris in Mattapan.

articles.title: OpenAI cyber models broke out of training environment to hack Hugging Face
articles.description: The incident is unique because it was "driven, end to end, by an autonomous AI agent system," according to Hugging Face.

articles.title: Samsung details features of its smart glasses as it enters market dominated by Meta
articles.description: The Samsung smart glasses are the tech giant's first foray into the product category which is currently dominated by Meta.

articles.title: Under-the-radar beverage name captures trader's attention
articles.description: Michael Khouw breaks down how he's trading this stock ahead of earnings.

articles.title: Nike to cut off thousands of online distributors in China, restructure digital footprint
articles.description: Nike is streamlining its online business in China as the company looks to stabilize pricing and branding and get back to growth.

articles.title: Trump's push for American-made AI chips hits TSMC's margins
articles.description: The Taiwan-based chipmaker has announced $200 billion in investment into U.S. manufacturing since Trump returned to power in 2025.

articles.title: AMD to invest up to $5 billion in Anthropic as part of computing power deal
articles.description: Anthropic's agreement with AMD is the latest in a string of infrastructure deals the company has announced this year to increase its computing capacity.

articles.title: Citizens United provision allowing foreign campaign contributions draws Democratic bill
articles.description: Rep. Jamie Raskin, D-Md., and Sen. Sheldon Whitehouse, D-R.I., want to establish foreign ownership thresholds for companies that spend on campaigns.

articles.title: Samsung ups prices as it launches new foldable smartphones ahead of expected Apple debut
articles.description: Samsung launched the Galaxy Z Fold8 Ultra, Galaxy Z Fold8 and Galaxy Z Flip8.

articles.title: The Fed rang the alarm about Anthropic's Mythos AI model — but had to go months without it
articles.description: The central bank didn't have access to Claude Mythos Preview as of mid-July, even as other institutions were racing to patch their vulnerabilities.

articles.title: Fidelity says 2026 retirees may spend $185,500 on healthcare. One category may push those costs higher
articles.description: A 65-year-old who retires in 2026 may spend an average of $185,500 on health and medical expenses in retirement, according to Fidelity Investments.

articles.title: Renting a yacht in the Mediterranean this summer just got cheaper
articles.description: Summer charters in the Med are down 20% to 30% from last year, yacht brokers say, with a growing number offering special discounts for July and August.

articles.title: Texas House Gets Rude Awakening as Small Plane Crashes Into Roof
articles.description: The plane, a Piper J3C-65, struck the house in Fulshear, Texas, around 6:45 a.m. on Monday, the authorities said. Its pilot was in serious condition on Tuesday, a hospital spokesman said.

articles.title: Mortgage rates are rising again, but homebuyers are seeing some advantages
articles.description: Mortgage rates last week rose to the highest level since last August, but homebuyer demand climbed as well, as buyers see more supply.

articles.title: Peacock is getting a lift from World Cup, Spanish-language content
articles.description: Peacock attracted new subscribers with the Spanish-language telecast of the World Cup, and executives say advertisers are taking notice.
